title: DeepWiki Coverage Expansion
intent_summary: Design an upstream coverage-expansion architecture that plans and renders a broader DeepWiki-style hierarchical wiki from repository evidence without weakening grounding.

target_system.name: wiki-generator
affected_pipeline: Phase 1 decomposition, condense, digest, bundle, retrieval substrate
Phase 4 grounded writing and generated coverage validation

requested_deliverables: Formal final technical design document with table of contents
Definition of done and validation report
ADR for hierarchical topic catalog decision

design_target.statement: Increase coverage by moving from a concise 22-section grounded wiki to a broader DeepWiki-style hierarchical page plan generated from repository evidence.
Add a repo-derived expanded topic/page catalog before Phase 2 planning.
design_target.grounding: sourced: user request, SRC-PRD UR-01, SRC-COVX Slice 1, SRC-DR

design_target.statement: Generate parent/child page plans from deterministic repo signals and LLM planning constrained by evidence-backed topic candidates.
Add per-page relevant file/symbol/span selection before Phase 3 evidence retrieval.
Retrieve evidence per page while keeping Phase 3 deterministic.
Render pages using the existing grounded claim/token plan path.
Evaluate coverage through topic→plan→evidence→output traceability, not benchmark copying.

hard_constraints.constraint: Benchmark material is comparison-only and must not become citeable evidence.
Strict validators must not be weakened to pass expanded output.
No output patching and no generic heal/retry loops.
Bounded repair is allowed only for LLM-authored page plans or claim plans, with exact diagnostics and hard caps.

hard_constraints.grounding: sourced: user request, SRC-PRD BR-05, SRC-SPEC

hard_constraints.constraint: Official final provider path remains Vertex/Gemini.
hard_constraints.grounding: sourced: user request and existing README/CLI

hard_constraints.constraint: Do not edit code and do not commit as part of this TDD task.

known_uncertainties.description: Exact source-derived thresholds for promoting a candidate topic/facet to required page status.
Whether expanded catalog enforcement becomes the default behavior of --coverage-mode enhancement immediately or rolls out in shadow/enforced stages.
Final page-count/topic-count target after the first real repository-derived catalog is generated.

Whether to model the new deterministic source-selection boundary as a new command or as part of normalize-plan. The design recommends integrating it into normalize-plan for compatibility, with an optional report-only command later.

assumptions.description: The current official generated wiki is valid for its current planned scope but insufficient for DeepWiki-style breadth.
The existing enhancement mode and grounded claim-plan implementation can be extended rather than replaced.
assumptions.grounding: inferred from SRC-SPEC and inspected code

assumptions.description: Most data contract changes can be additive to existing SectionPlan/EvidencePacket/generated-coverage artifacts to preserve baseline mode.
assumptions.grounding: inferred from existing normalized plan and coverage artifacts
